Empires & Puzzles Basil Review: Is The Red Rat Worth It?

Basil is a strange little creature. He tries to combine the safety of shared damage with the violence of counterattack. Does this contradictory kit actually work, or does he just trip over his own tail? We break down the stats and strategy.
Basil: The Red Rat That Wants To Be Gunnar With Teeth

family, originally released in the Covenant Summon portal around Christmas 2022. He represents a very specific archetype: the "Support Tank." Visually, he's a battle-hardened rat warrior who looks like he's seen things—terrible things, mostly involving mousetraps and hungry cats.

His kit is a cocktail of defensive buffs and offensive retaliation, making him a "Red Gunnar" with a twist. While he lacks a costume (unlike his blue counterpart who has two), Basil tries to carve out a niche by making your team unkillable while simultaneously punishing the enemy for trying. He is primarily a Rush Attack specialist, as his Slow speed makes him a sitting duck in standard formats.

Basil sports a gritty, "Redwall-meets-Mad-Max" aesthetic. He wields a massive axe and a wooden shield, adorned with tribal skulls and armor scraps. It's a surprisingly serious look for a rodent, contrasting with the often cartoonish designs of other animal heroes. You get the sense that Basil isn't just a pest; he's a pest with a plan and a very large axe.

Quite low, but he's a support hero, so this is forgivable.

This is where the power creep really hurts. Modern 3-star heroes like
(908 HP) simply have much larger health pools. Basil relies heavily on his defensive buffs to mitigate this lower HP.
Basil's special skill is a "kitchen sink" of defensive mechanics, but it suffers from a bit of an identity crisis.
This is his strongest asset. Sharing damage among all allies prevents snipers from one-shotting your key heroes. It essentially turns your team into one giant health bar.
A ramping defense buff is fantastic for long battles. It makes the shared damage even more manageable.
Counterattack damage depends on the damage you take.
By buffing your defense and sharing the damage (which mitigates spikes), you are actively reducing the damage your enemies take from the counterattack.
While this "anti-synergy" frustrates min-maxers, it actually serves a purpose:

The counterattack shouldn't be viewed as a nuke, but rather as a "thorn" deterrent. The primary goal is to keep your team alive; the damage reflection is just a nice bonus that chips away at the enemy.
Slow. This means 12 tiles to charge. In Rush Attack tournaments, this becomes Very Fast (6.5 tiles), which is where Basil shines.
A +65% defense buff combined with shared damage reduces incoming damage significantly—often by more than 50-60%.

If an enemy hits for 100 damage (after defense reduction), the team takes 100 damage total (20 per hero). The counterattack reflects 70-100 damage back. It's not huge per hit, but against AoE enemies, it adds up.

If Basil is hit with a defense down or mana ailment, firing his skill throws that problem right back at the enemy. If he's clean, he gets a nice self-sustain heal.
A generic but useful power for a tank. It helps him survive the initial barrage before he can get his buffs up.

Upon release, Basil was immediately dubbed the "Red Gunnar." Players were confused by the counter-intuitive skill set (Def Up + Riposte), but he quickly found a home in Rush tournaments where Red tanks were needed.

offer higher raw survivability or more aggressive threats. However, the specific combination of
Basil is effectively retired from standard play. He is too slow and his stats are too low. However, in
3-Star Rush Attack Tournaments (especially buff booster or no-blue)
, he is still a top-tier tank or flank option.
Basil's job is to survive until he casts. Once he casts, his job is to stay alive to keep the buffs active. Maximizing Defense and Health is the only logical choice.
Lowers his counterattack damage slightly (since he takes less damage), but a dead Basil reflects zero damage.

In 3-star Rush tournaments, he creates a defensive wall that is incredibly hard for uncoordinated teams to break.
Shared damage completely neutralizes enemy snipers, forcing them to chew through the entire team's HP pool.
The self-heal/cleanse transfer passive adds a layer of sophistication that older 3-stars lack.
Being Red allows him to tank effectively in "No Blue" tournaments where Gunnar is banned or weak.

His absolute best use case. Put him at Tank or Flank.
He provides multiple buffs (Def, Counterattack, Soul Connect), powering up the team's attack.
Can be used to keep squishy damage dealers alive against bosses, though items usually do this better.

If you pull Basil early, he is a safe investment
. He will help you survive difficult map stages by keeping your damage dealers alive. However, do not prioritize him over fast snipers or healers for your general raiding team.
Basil is a "keep one for tournaments" hero. You likely won't use him anywhere else. If you have a fully emblemed/limit-broken
, you might find Basil collecting dust. However, in
, he is superior to Gunnar simply because he isn't weak to the strong color (Green) that attackers will bring.
When Basil dropped, the community was... confused.
"First I saw red and slow and was going to stop reading... Counterattack first then damage share and defense boost? In the current order, it’s totally counterproductive."
Many players initially dismissed him as a
However, once Rush tournaments hit, opinions shifted:
"I laughed at your face, I played you down... But you’re so good. Your shared damage and counterattack carried me to a 5-0 today. Forgive me."
The consensus settled on him being a specialist tool:
"Only potential use case I see in the rat’s current form is for no blue rush tourneys."

Basil is a paradox: a defensive hero with an offensive bite that dulls his own teeth. While his stats are gathering dust and his speed is a liability, he remains a staple in the specific niche of 3-Star Rush Tournaments. He isn't flashy, and he isn't a "must-have" generalist, but when the conditions are right, this little rat can build a fortress that few can breach.
Level him for Rush Tournaments, but don't expect him to carry you through the daily grind.
